prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, my dear! A CPU is like the brain of the computer—it’s the part that thinks and figures things out. When you click on a game or type a message, the CPU is the busy little worker that makes everything happen. It doesn’t store your pictures or songs—that’s what memory and hard drives are for—but it does all the quick thinking to run them. Think of it like the chef in a kitchen: it follows recipes (those are the instructions from programs) and puts everything together just right. Even though I don’t quite get all the techy bits, I know it’s mighty important—just like how your brain helps you play, learn, and giggle!
